title: tephrosia punctata subsp. punctata
intro: Tephrosia punctata subsp. punctata, also known as spotted tephrosia, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family that is native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and to treat various health conditions, including fever and rheumatism. The health benefits of spotted tephrosia include its potential to reduce blood pressure and to have anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas and infusions that can be used to treat a range of ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
